what: THE PROBE CORPUS. The questions the system ASKS — reusable, fired deterministically at nexuses over varying content, each carrying a measured YIELD. Not a list of open items. A record here is a question that will be asked again; a question asked once is not a corpus member (see `the_one_kind` below).
the_one_kind:
A member is a PROBE: a question fired repeatedly at a nexus, whose value is measurable across firings. THE DISCRIMINATOR IS YIELD, and it is mechanical rather than a judgment call — yield means 'how often did this question produce a hypothesis that survived verification' (MAP.md:262), and that is only computable for something fired more than once. If yield is measurable, it belongs here. If yield is meaningless because the question is asked once and then done, it is a HELD NODE — an intention in fill-state that has not cast — and it belongs with nodes, not in a corpus that exists to measure repetition.

This store held nothing for eight days precisely because that line was never drawn: Akien, 2026-07-22 — 'we didn't have questions because you kept using it for multiple kinds of things.' A store with two kinds in it cannot be chartered, because its charter has to describe two things and therefore describes neither.
why:
Telos 4's compilable core: 'the answers vary, but the questions do not.' A fixed, evolving question set fired deterministically over variable content is what makes the resolver's work compressible — the probe is the reusable part, the answer is the novel part, and Law 1 says the resolver is spent on the novel. T4.1 makes the corpus a chartered store with yield tracking; T5.1 makes tenure earned and losers retired. Both are unstarted today, and this store is what starts them.

Why it matters that probes become FIRST-CLASS rather than staying embedded in skill files: a probe inside `/intent`'s markdown is asked but never measured, so no probe has ever been retired for uselessness and no probe has ever earned tenure. The whole promotion/retirement mechanism — Telos 5 in measurable form (MAP.md:264) — has no substrate until the probes are records. MAP.md:665 puts the start of that data 'from the first /sorted onward, months before any intuition device'; we are past the first /sorted by many and have collected none.
owner: each probe has exactly one owner who gates writes to it (Law 6). The store owns the collection.

template.owner: the one owner who gates writes to this probe
template.probe: the question as fired, phrased so it can be asked of content it has never seen
template.fired_at: which nexus fires it (/intent, /sorted's completeness gate, /challenge, …) and at what point in the sequence
template.why: what failure this probe catches — required. A probe that cannot name what it catches has no way to earn tenure.
template.yield: the measure: firings, and of those, how many produced a hypothesis that survived verification. Starts unmeasured and says so (Law 3).
template.tenure: CANDIDATE | TENURED | RETIRED — earned by yield, not by age or by who proposed it

notes_on_the_store: An answered question that becomes a commitment turns into an INTENTION node (MAP.md:587). That transition applies to held nodes, not to probes — a probe is never 'answered', it is fired again. Keeping the two kinds apart is what makes that sentence unambiguous.
A retired probe is not deleted. It freezes with its yield record, because the measurement that retired it is the evidence for why we stopped asking (Law 7).

The founding members are not new questions. They are the probes already being fired from inside skill files — /intent's five, /sorted's four coverage questions — which have been asked dozens of times and measured zero times. Extracting them is the store's first job, not inventing more.
no_drain_yet: IOU, and a load-bearing one. Akien, 2026-07-22: 'if you're going to post feedback to questions, then we will need an overnight process to process them.' A corpus with an intake and no drain is worse than no corpus — it is the APPEARANCE of capture, which is the failure this system exists to stop. Nothing computes yield today, so nothing earns tenure and nothing retires; the store is an accumulator until the drain exists. Tracked as a node (notes/overnight-drain.json), un-cast, and a candidate first member of `operational-driver` — a class defined 2026-07-18 with zero members, which by its own definition means the class is unproven.
provenance: Founded 2026-07-22, then REWRITTEN the same hour. The first version was invented from scratch and got the kind wrong — it chartered the store around /sorted kick-backs, made `reds` an optional field (the tell: an optional field means two shapes in one store), and re-derived a spec that MAP.md already held at 4.1, 5.1, 262-264, and 587. Akien caught it. Recorded rather than quietly corrected, because 'the store's own charter re-derived a settled answer' is exactly the defect Law 1 names, committed inside the artifact meant to prevent it.
